text:
From: Elena Gilmore <elena@gilmore.ai>
To: Aditya Rao <aditya.rao@redwood.ai>
Cc: Greg Wallace <greg.wallace@gilmore.ai>
Date: Sun, 18 Oct 2026 09:14:00 -0700
Subject: Sudden latency + error spike after v3.1-quant rollout

Aditya —

We pushed your v3.1-quant model yesterday evening (kanban release window) and starting overnight we’re seeing a major change in production: token p95 latency is ~5x baseline, and our request error rate climbed from ~0.5% to ~12%. These errors are decode-time timeouts and some "model not available" during peak bursts. This is affecting our customer-facing summarization flows; we’ve paused non-critical jobs, but the real-time API used by our mobile app is impacted and we risk churn.

Attached: a short sample of error logs and a capture of a failed request (gilmore-error-sample-20261018.log). We rotated keys and retried to rule out auth. The only change on ou...

Claire — great talking earlier. Per our call, FluxHealth is looking to add ~30% extra committed capacity mid-contract and prefers not to move the renewal date. Two things I want to propose so we can get legal and finance aligned quickly:

1) Staged billing: split the incremental uplift across 3 invoices over the remaining 9 months (monthly amortization on the uplift portion) so cashflow stays predictable on your side.

2) Discount ladder: a blended uplift discount of 8% on the additional QPS if you commit to the staged payment schedule, or 5% if you prefer a one-time midcycle invoice.

Hi Lauren,

We're seeing a sharp regression in p95/p99 latency on the Hosted API that's spiking our checkout times and caus...
text:
From: Alicia Gomez <agomez@furnishly.com>
To: Lauren Bishop <lauren_bishop@redwood.com>
Date: 2026-08-17T09:03:00-07:00
Subject: Realtime: Hosted API latency spike impacting Furnishly checkout

Hi Lauren,

We're seeing a sharp regression in p95/p99 latency on the Hosted API that's spiking our checkout times and causing payment timeouts. Impact started ~08:52 PDT. Quick numbers from our monitoring: p95 went from ~120ms to ~1.1s, p99 from ~240ms to ~2.8s, error rate jumped from 0.2% to ~2.4% over the last 15 minutes. Throughput for our integration has dipped ~18% as requests back off.

Sample request id: req_9f3b2a (from 2026-08-17T08:54:12Z). We can reproduce on multiple regions (us-west1 primarily). This is customer-facing and blocking checkout flows for ~6% of sessions.

We urgently need a triage and a mitigation plan. Attaching a small snippet of our request logs and a synthetic tra...

Subject: Integration rehearsal: temporary runway & autoscale exception request

Hi team,

Quick summary: Orion Health is planning a multi-week integration rehearsal (end-to-end traffic with production-like payloads) to validate cold-start behavior, cache warming, and billing events ahead of their public launch. They want to run two sustained weekends of high-concurrency tests starting the weekend of Aug 14.

Why we need an exception:
- Expected steady-state QPS: 1,200 reqs/s during peak windows (short bursts to 2,000).
- Model: r-infer-13B (FP16) with KV cache; warm-up time for cold starts is ~18s without prefix cache.
- Primary concern: cold-start tail latency causing false-positive S...
